summary:
The reviewed literature provides no direct evidence on how a surgeon’s prior experience affects their ability to perform transurethral resection of the prostate. Due to this absence of specific data, it is appropriate to conclude that the relationship remains uncertain—hence "maybe." 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically pertinent aspects related to surgery skills and procedure efficacy as they pertain directly to the query asked.

Second must emphasize why experience isn't addressed—the missing link between operator background and actual procedural capability—and thus leads logically to an answer reflecting insufficiency rather than affirmation or denial.

Avoid adding external knowledge; stay grounded entirely in content from the rationale itself.
